verification_gate: Every personal data class has explicit ownership, consent, storage, encryption, access, retention, export, recovery, and proof tasks; no data leaves its boundary silently.

personal_data_classes_enumerated: PERSONAL-DC-11 Personal agent cognition & chat memory
gate_result.classes_derived_from: data.js personal workspace sections + Settings/Profile panels, cross-referenced with LIFEOS-008 capability list (identity, privacy, memory, planning, health, finance, legal, files, calendar, contacts, communications) and INTEL-001 data-substrate classes (relational, vector, graph, embedded-ledger, artifact, identity, provenance, hot/warm/cold memory)

clause_ownership.value: Each of 12 classes names exactly one user_authority (sovereign rights-holder) and one store_owner: PostgreSQL+RuVector (DC-01,03,04,05,06,07,08,10,12), OS keyring (DC-02), AgentDB .rvf (DC-11), redb-terminal for raw presence then PostgreSQL for settled records (DC-09). No class has two durable writers.

clause_consent.value: Every class has an explicit consent gate; DC-09 (family presence, incl. minors) requires multi-party/guardian consent; DC-02 secret use is per-use scoped; ingestion/sharing without a logged grant is barred and every grant/revocation is recorded to PERSONAL-DC-12.

clause_storage.value: Every class maps to a STORE-001 state class: durable relational (DC-01,03,04,05,06,07,08,10,12), semantic/vector/graph in RuVector-in-PostgreSQL (DC-03,08,10), transient high-frequency in redb (DC-03,04,07,09,10,11), per-agent AgentDB .rvf (DC-01,11), derived envctl projection (DC-06), external-secret keyring (DC-02,10,12). Local-first = canonical durable owner runs on the user's own edge node.

clause_encryption.value: Every class declares an at-rest boundary with key custody in the OS keyring: PostgreSQL durable data encrypted at rest under keyring-held keys; secrets (DC-02, DC-04/DC-10 tokens) live only in the keyring; redb buffers are local memory-mapped files never networked; AgentDB is a single local .rvf. No plaintext secret ever reaches PostgreSQL or redb.

clause_access.value: Every class scopes access to user + explicitly authorized agents; RuVector MinCut deterministically isolates any drifting/hallucinating agent before it reads sensitive rows; DC-09 gates minors' location behind guardian consent; DC-12 access is user-readable audit.

clause_retention.value: Every class has a retention tier: durable classes retained per user-set tier with PostgreSQL PITR history (finance/legal long-horizon); transient classes (DC-03 raw samples, DC-09 raw presence, DC-11 chat) explicitly do NOT persist raw streams; DC-12 ledger is append-only long-horizon.

clause_export.value: Every class defines export/sharing as an explicit envctl projection (DC-06 filesystem, others record/artifact) gated by a witnessed consent grant; secrets are non-exportable in bulk (DC-02); no background/automatic outbound sync exists for any class.

clause_recovery.value: Every durable class has a recovery source: PostgreSQL PITR (DC-01,03,04,05,06,07,08,10,12), OS keyring + committed pins (DC-02), AgentDB regenerable from base model + PostgreSQL-rooted history (DC-11), exact-byte SHA-256 re-materialization for artifacts (DC-05,06). Transient classes (raw presence/samples/chat) declare non-recoverable-by-design. Backup declared per class.

clause_proof_tasks.value: 10 enumerated proof tasks (PERSONAL-001-PT-OWNERSHIP, -CONSENT, -STORAGE-TIER, -ENCRYPTION, -RETENTION, -RECOVERY, -EGRESS, -SECRET-ISOLATION, -PROVENANCE, -EPHEMERAL-CHAT); every class's work_item.proof_task references the tasks that verify its contract dimensions.

clause_no_silent_egress.value: Enforced structurally by PERSONAL-DC-12, a durable append-only PostgreSQL provenance/consent/egress ledger: every consent, access, cross-boundary movement, and deletion on DC-01..11 must write a SHAKE256-witnessed, Causal-Graph-verified entry before crossing a boundary. Reinforced by forward-only envctl sync (no undirected dual-write), No-Sidecar RuVector (no external vector store to exfiltrate from), and the keyring hard boundary (secrets never egress into PostgreSQL/redb/projections/network).

store_001_alignment.value: PostgreSQL+RuVector is the single canonical durable owner of personal relational + semantic macro-state; redb is the transient high-frequency tier (presence stream, raw samples, mention/notification buffers, agent scratchpad); AgentDB .rvf is per-agent non-authoritative cognition; OS keyring is the unchanged external-secret authority; envctl is the forward-only bridge + projection engine. Any redb-durable personal class migrates to PostgreSQL only via a verified BlobStore SHA-256 parity cutover, never by assumption.

proof_summary: PERSONAL-001 defines the personal identity, privacy, and data-ownership contract for LifeOS by enumerating the 12 real personal data classes drawn from the shipped personal app surfaces (data.js personal workspace: Family, Finance, Health, Legal, Files, Calendar, Wallet, Social Media, Contacts; plus the Settings/Profile Logins-and-passwords and Secrets-and-keys panels and the AIAvatar identity model) cross-referenced with the LIFEOS-008 capability list and the INTEL-001 data-substrate classes. Each class (PERSONAL-DC-01 Identity, DC-02 Credentials/secrets, DC-03 Health, DC-04 Finance/wallet ledger, DC-05 Legal/estate, DC-06 Files/vault, DC-07 Calendar/planning, DC-08 Contacts/relationship graph, DC-09 Family presence/shared hub, DC-10 Social/communications, DC-11 Agent cognition/chat, DC-12 Provenance/consent/egress ledger) is assigned exactly once against the STORE-001 tiers: PostgreSQL+RuVector owns durable relational + semantic macro-state (identity of record, ledger, legal docs, file content, calendar, contacts+GNN relationship graph, message records, and the audit ledger); the OS keyring is the sole authority for raw credentials, passkeys, API/SSH/PGP keys, and account link tokens, which never touch PostgreSQL or redb; redb is the transient owner of raw family presence/location, raw biometric samples, mention/notification buffers, and agent scratchpad, with only settled significant states flushed forward by envctl; AgentDB .rvf holds the personal agent's non-authoritative MicroLoRA/SONA cognition witnessed back to PostgreSQL source vectors, while live chat (aiMessages) is deliberately ephemeral per the CLAUDE.md persistence whitelist; the personal file browser is a derived, disposable envctl projection into the locked $APPDATA/lifeos scope. Every class carries user-sovereign ownership, an explicit (multi-party where shared) consent gate, an at-rest encryption boundary with keyring key custody, MinCut-guarded access, a retention tier, a witnessed export/sharing path, a deletion authority with tombstoning, a backup, and a recovery source, plus references to the 10 enumerated proof tasks. 'No data leaves its boundary silently' is enforced structurally by PERSONAL-DC-12, a durable append-only PostgreSQL provenance/consent/egress ledger that records a SHAKE256-witnessed, Causal-Graph-verified entry for every consent, access, cross-boundary movement, and deletion before it happens, reinforced by forward-only envctl sync, No-Sidecar RuVector-in-PostgreSQL, and the keyring secret isolation boundary. The design aligns with the STORE-001 keystone (PostgreSQL canonical durable, redb transient, verified SHA-256 cutover for any redb-durable migration) and completes the intended Blueprint architecture for the personal domain. This is a planning/definition artifact only: no product code was written, no schema deployed, no personal data or credential accessed, and no account mutated (product_execution_performed=false).
inputs_grounded_in: 1.0_VISION/Notebooklm/Architecture Context Matrix_ PostgreSQL-First LifeOS Framework - Table 1.csv
